Experiment is the same, normalization differs.
Data-acquisition system based on 100 MHz FLASH-ADC.
512 TOF channels x 128 pulse height channels array
was accumulated for each target nucleus.
TOF and pulse-height spectra were accumulated as
1 GeV pulsed proton beam strikes lead target.
Beam is shaped by system of iron, brass, lead
collimators.Swipping magnet at 30m from neutron source.
Average intensity 3.E+14 n/s, burst duration 10 ns,
EN-RSL - energy resolution of GNEIS facility < 0.5 %.
Two fast parallel plates ionization chambers,
filled with methane working gas at pressure 3 atm.
Start signal was provided by gamma-flash detector.
Painting technique with high quality fissionable
Fission event counting rates were corrected for:
- neutron flux attenuation for investigated and
reference nuclei (using total c-s of ENDF/B-VI),
- fragment loses in targets due finite deposit
- neutron momentum transfer and angular anisotropy of
measurements were normalized using target thickness.
